findings.rewrite: Espresso shaken ice-cold with vodka and coffee liqueur.
Crisp, golden beer-battered cod over proper chips with mushy peas — the Dockside classic.
Teriyaki-glazed salmon resting on stir-fried vegetables, with steamed rice.
Chunky beef slow-simmered in rich Guinness gravy under a crisp pastry lid, with mash and veg.
Layers of fresh ground beef, creamy cheese sauce and pasta sheets, served with chips.
A giant rack glazed in our homemade BBQ sauce, with chips and salad — bring napkins.
Our most tender cut from the grill, with chips, salad and homemade peppercorn sauce.
Crisp-edged smash patty with melted cheese, bacon and BBQ sauce, lettuce and tomato — chips included.

summary: Overall menu score: 67/100. Of 192 priced items, 90 classify as stars, 98 as plowhorses, 1 as puzzles and 2 as dogs (estimated mode — based on documented priors, not your sales data).
Price architecture: 5 categories break Omnes' 3× dispersion guideline. 81 price adjustments are recommended, each tied to an explicit rule.
Copy quality scores 59/100. No blocking compliance issues were detected.
quickWins: Rework or retire the 2 dog items — they occupy space without earning it.
Apply the 81 recommended price moves — all are small, rule-based and reversible.
Rewrite bare/generic descriptions with sensory, specific language — measured uplift in the literature is meaningful.
Add a visible allergen note to every menu format you print.
